title: alchemilla vulgaris subsp. tenuis
intro: Alchemilla vulgaris subsp. tenuis, commonly known as lady's mantle, is a subspecies of the lady's mantle plant, a perennial herb native to Europe and Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which can help to reduce swelling and soothe skin irritations. The health benefits of Alchemilla vulgaris subsp. tenuis include its ability to treat digestive issues, menopausal symptoms, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne, while also exhibiting antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of the plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
